word: G3424 - μογιλάλος - mogilálos - mog-il-al'-os - Having an impediment in his speech

sections.paragraph: Short Definition: Having an impediment in his speech

- Original: μογιλάλος

- Transliteration: Mogilalos

- Phonetic: mog-il-al'-os

- Definition:

1. speaking with difficulty

- Origin: from G3425 and G2980

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G3425 and G2980; hardly talking that is dumb (tongue tied): - having an impediment in his speech.
